title: Oxford, Watlington & London Design Show

main_route.original_wording: Oxford, Frenchay Rd off Woodstock Rd
main_route.excerpt: Simon, Finder & Eve drove to Charles' & Jenny's new house in Oxford, Frenchay Rd off Woodstock Rd.
On to deliver lights Watlington & [?send] Hinley.
On to Peter & Nana's house at Finsbury Pk. We slept in van & had breakfast with Nana & Thomas.
We drove on & found a carpark next to St. Mary's Headley & Box Hill, a well-kept church, for lunch.
We are at a farm above Steyning.
At △ we left for our campsite The Ship & Anchor at Littlehampton ***

main_route.inference_reason: All trips start and end at home; mixed_end boundary but return is standard assumption

excursions.label: Merchant Taylors BBQ and design event (Wednesday evening)

stops.excerpt: We went to the Merchant Taylors, gorgeous, & had a BBQ in courtyard put on by [illegible]. We (Chad) had invited 3 designers.

excursions.label: End of year London Design Show (Thursday)

stops.excerpt: We went to the end of year London Show. Jason (the designer's husband) gave us tickets. It was WOW!

We visited Simon & Claudia Ogden for elevenses. They were very welcoming. Then we joined the Gravestocks who gave us a delicious lunch. We went for a walk through a park & watched water skiing & saw the sea.

excursions.label: Cycling to Arundel and Chichester, Evensong, Bognor Regis (Saturday)

stops.excerpt: We set off, cycling along the raised embankment next to the R. Arun. We could see Arundel Castle in the distance.
We caught a train back to Ford Station along the road to the campsite pub for lunch... After a nap we drove to Chichester & looked at the shops & went to Evensong.
We stopped at Bognor Regis (George V bugger Bognor!) on the way back to the campsite for supper in the van.

unresolved.issue: Mentioned after Watlington in the context of delivering lights: '[?send] Hinley'. Could be a person's name rather than a place, or a misread of Henley (on Thames). Cannot confirm as a stop.
unresolved.excerpt: On to deliver lights Watlington & [?send] Hinley.

unresolved.issue: Visited after Headley/Box Hill lunch stop, en route south. Location not stated. 'Eng 12. & Evans 10' may refer to their children's ages or names. Cannot geolocate.
unresolved.excerpt: On to see Arathna & Godfrey. Eng 12. & Evans 10, spent all our visit playing with Tinder.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: preceding trip content on B_0267_L (Gloucester-Hereford canal, Ashperton tunnel note, Sunday church at St. G.)
mixed_end: trip ends mid-volume B_0269_R; return to Birmingham is inferred
summary: Simon and Eve (with the dog Tinder) made a van trip south from Birmingham in July 2016: visiting Charles and Jenny's new house in Oxford, delivering lights at Watlington, staying overnight at Peter and Nana's in Finsbury Park for a design BBQ at Merchant Taylors and a design show. They then drove south via Box Hill to a farm campsite above Steyning, then on to The Ship & Anchor campsite at Littlehampton, from which they made a cycling excursion along the Arun to Arundel and Chichester Cathedral for Evensong.
extraction_notes: Pages B_0267_L has mixed start with a preceding trip (Gloucester-Hereford canal entry). The date 'Wednesday July 6th' maps to 2016-07-06. No index entry available. 'Finder' in the text is Tinder the dog. The end date is uncertain as boundary is mixed; Saturday July 9 (2016-07-09) is the last clearly datable day. 'Hinley' is ambiguous — likely a misread or informal reference rather than a geocodable place. The Arathna & Godfrey visit location is unresolved. The London Design Show venue is not named precisely. The Chichester Cathedral prayer caption confirms the Chichester visit. Ford Station is on the Littlehampton-Chichester rail line, consistent with the cycling narrative.
